description_long: This is a traditional Japanese samurai helmet, known as a kabuto, featuring a dark, ribbed dome with a prominent golden crescent-shaped crest (maedate) at the front. The helmet is adorned with side flaps (fukigaeshi) and a segmented neck guard (shikoro) that extends down the back, all rendered in a dark metallic finish. The face is protected by a menacing red-brown menpo (facial mask) with a fierce expression, adding to its intimidating appearance. The golden crescent crest is highly polished and contrasts with the matte black of the helmet body. The surface of the helmet is mostly smooth with subtle texturing on the dome and neck guard, while the mask has a slightly rougher, matte finish. This helmet is an ornate and historical piece, likely used for ceremonial or display purposes rather than modern protection.
description: A traditional Japanese samurai kabuto helmet with a dark ribbed dome, golden crescent crest, and a fierce red-brown facial mask.
description_view_0: Front view showing the golden crescent crest, fierce red-brown facial mask, and side flaps.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the ribbed dome, side flap, and layered neck guard.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the segmented neck guard and the full span of the golden crest.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, showing the helmet's profile and mask edge.
